columns.content: Human-readable description of what the FR requires. Synthesizes FR title and key ACs into a coherent paragraph — not just IDs.

What the implementation touches and how it impacts the system. Drawn from architecture-overview.md and module-design.md. Describes affected modules, contracts, patterns, and behavioral changes — not just file paths.
TNNN task IDs with brief descriptions. One line per task. Format: TNNN description-fragment

scoping_rules.tasks: Primary view: FR → Requirement description → Design impact → Task list. Used at PRESENTING_TASKS confirmation.
scoping_rules.requirements: FR → Requirement description → (design column omitted or shows 'pending') → (tasks column omitted or shows 'pending')
scoping_rules.architecture: FR → Requirement description → Design impact → (tasks column shows 'pending')
scoping_rules.design: FR → Requirement description → Design impact → (tasks column shows 'pending')

content_guidance.requirement_column: Write 2-4 sentences describing what the FR requires in plain language. Include the core behavior change and why it matters. Do NOT list AC IDs — weave the acceptance criteria into the description naturally.
content_guidance.design_column: Write 2-4 sentences describing what changes in the codebase. Name the affected modules, patterns, and contracts. Explain the blast radius — what else is impacted by this change. Do NOT list file paths — describe the architectural impact.
content_guidance.tasks_column: List each related task as 'TNNN brief-description' on its own line. Keep descriptions to 3-5 words. Group by logical area if many tasks.
